server.name: acc-forma-mcp-server
server.description: MCP server exposing read and governed-write access to Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC) and Autodesk Platform Services (APS) APIs — Data Management, Issues, Reviews, AEC Data Model, Model Derivative, Model Coordination, and Model Properties — for use by LLM agents in AEC/BIM QC and coordination workflows.

tools.name: admin_list_projects
tools.description: List the projects under a hub with account-admin metadata (status, type, dates, address), optionally filtered by status.

tools.name: admin_get_project
tools.description: Fetch a single project's account-admin metadata: status, type, job number, address, and start/end dates.

tools.name: admin_list_users
tools.description: List the members of an account, returning user IDs, names, roles, and account profile fields. Account-level directory only.

tools.name: admin_list_companies
tools.description: List companies registered on the account.

tools.name: dm_list_hubs
tools.description: List ACC/BIM 360 hubs accessible to the authenticated app.

tools.name: dm_list_projects
tools.description: List projects within a given hub.

tools.name: dm_list_top_folders
tools.description: List the top-level folders of a project.

tools.name: dm_list_folder_contents
tools.description: List items — files and subfolders — inside a folder.

tools.name: dm_get_item
tools.description: Get metadata for a single item (a file/document), including its tip version ID.

tools.name: dm_list_versions
tools.description: List all versions of a file with version numbers and timestamps.

tools.name: issues_list
tools.description: List issues in a project, filterable by status or assignee.

tools.name: issues_get
tools.description: Get full details for a single issue.

tools.name: issues_create
tools.description: Create a new issue, including subtype, location, due date, and assignment.

tools.name: issues_pin_element
tools.description: Create an issue with a 3D pushpin automatically placed on a specific BIM element.

tools.name: issues_update
tools.description: Update fields on an existing issue via a sparse (partial) update.

tools.name: issues_add_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to an existing issue.

tools.name: issues_list_comments
tools.description: List the comment thread on an issue, paginated.

tools.name: issues_list_types
tools.description: List the valid issue types and subtypes configured for a project.

tools.name: issues_list_root_causes
tools.description: List the configured root-cause categories for a project.

tools.name: issues_get_user_me
tools.description: Get the current identity's permission flags for the Issues module.

tools.name: issues_list_attrs
tools.description: List custom attribute definitions configured for a project's issues.

tools.name: issues_list_attachments
tools.description: List files and links attached to an issue, paginated.

tools.name: reviews_list
tools.description: List reviews in a project's Reviews container.

tools.name: reviews_get
tools.description: Get a single review, including its status and assigned reviewers.

tools.name: reviews_create
tools.description: Create a new review with reviewers, due date, and workflow type.

tools.name: reviews_transition
tools.description: Transition a review's state: submit, approve, reject, void, or reopen.

tools.name: aecdm_list_hubs
tools.description: List AEC Data Model (AECDM)-native hubs.

tools.name: aecdm_list_projects
tools.description: List projects within an AECDM hub.

tools.name: aecdm_list_element_groups
tools.description: List BIM model files published to an AECDM project.

tools.name: aecdm_list_categories
tools.description: List BIM element categories present in an element group.

tools.name: aecdm_query_elements
tools.description: Query BIM elements by category, returning their full property sets.

tools.name: aecdm_get_element_properties
tools.description: Re-fetch the full property set for a specific element by node ID.

tools.name: aecdm_aggregate_by_parameter
tools.description: Count elements grouped by a given parameter value within a category.

tools.name: aecdm_query_element_positions
tools.description: Query elements together with their origin position (x, y, z) in metres, used to place Issue pushpins.

tools.name: md_get_manifest
tools.description: Check SVF2 translation status for a model version and list available viewable GUIDs.

tools.name: md_get_properties
tools.description: Fetch Revit element properties from an SVF2-translated model.

tools.name: md_trigger_translation
tools.description: Submit a new SVF2 translation job for a specific model version.

tools.name: mc_list_modelsets
tools.description: List the coordination modelsets configured in a project.

tools.name: mc_list_clashes
tools.description: Retrieve clash-detection results for a coordination modelset.

tools.name: mp_diff_versions
tools.description: Diff two versions of the same model, returning added/removed/modified element statistics by category.

tools.name: docs_get_viewables
tools.description: Resolve the ACC Docs-native viewable ID for a document version URN.

tools.name: webhooks_list
tools.description: List the webhooks this application has registered, with each hook's id, event, delivery status, callback URL and scope.

tools.name: webhooks_create
tools.description: Register a webhook so Autodesk posts an event payload to a customer-specified callback URL whenever the subscribed Data Management or Issues event occurs.

tools.name: webhooks_delete
tools.description: Delete a webhook, permanently stopping event delivery to its callback URL.

tools.name: meta_list_changelog
tools.description: Read local audit-log entries for one date, filterable by tool name and audit stage.

tools.name: meta_verify_audit_chain
tools.description: Verify that the local audit log's hash chain has not been tampered with.

external_endpoints.purpose: Autodesk Platform Services (APS) REST and GraphQL APIs: Account Admin, Data Management, Construction Issues, Construction Reviews, AEC Data Model, Model Derivative, Model Coordination, and Model Properties.

Autodesk-issued pre-signed S3 URLs (returned by the Model Coordination clash API) for downloading clash-result resource files. URLs are short-lived (~60s TTL), issued by Autodesk, and fetched without any bearer token. No data is uploaded.

Disclosed because webhooks_create configures an outbound data flow this server does not itself make. When an operator registers a webhook, Autodesk — not this server — posts event payloads (file and folder metadata, or issue contents) directly to the callback URL the operator supplied, and continues to do so until the hook is deleted. This server never posts to that host and never fetches it; it only registers and deletes the subscription. The host is constrained by the FORMA_ALLOWED_CALLBACK_HOSTS allow-list, must be https, and may not be a loopback, private or link-local address.

autodesk_apis_used: Authentication API (OAuth2 v2 — /authentication/v2/token, used to mint SSA and 2LO access tokens)
Webhooks API v1 (2LO — registering, listing and deleting event subscriptions for Data Management and Construction Issues events)

auth.notes: SSA is a bot identity scoped per-project via Hub Admin > Custom Integrations; it is required for user-context APIs (Issues, Reviews, AEC Data Model, Model Coordination) and is the default for everything else. When APS_AUTH_MODE=ssa the server also mints a 2LO (app-only client-credentials) token, and the Account Admin, Data Management, Model Derivative and Docs viewables read tools use that one, because 2LO sees every project in the hub while SSA sees only the projects it is assigned to. The choice is made per tool at startup and is not a failover: a failed 2LO request is not retried under SSA. When APS_AUTH_MODE=2lo, no SSA token is minted and the tools requiring SSA are disabled. Credentials are supplied by the customer via environment variables; the server ships with no credentials. End-user OAuth (3LO) is not implemented in this release; it is planned for a later phase.

security_controls: Two-step mutation flow (default FORMA_MUTATION_MODE=preview_required): a dry-run preview plus a payload-bound, single-use approval token are required before any mutating tool executes

Operator-configurable mutation modes, disclosed here: preview_required (default, two-step), client_approval_only (the operator's MCP client is trusted to approve; the two-step token flow is skipped), readonly (all mutations disabled)

Hub/project allow-list (FORMA_ALLOWED_HUBS, FORMA_ALLOWED_PROJECTS) restricting which resources the server may touch. Every tool declares how it binds to the allow-list, and the binding is enforced centrally: tools that take a Data Management hub/project id are checked against it; discovery tools (dm_list_hubs, dm_list_projects, admin_list_projects) additionally filter their own results down to allow-listed entries. Tools addressed by an id that cannot be mapped back to a DM hub/project — a Model Derivative URN, or an AEC Data Model-native id, neither of whose endpoints are project-scoped — are refused outright while an allow-list is active, rather than allowed through unchecked; this covers all AEC Data Model tools, md_get_manifest, md_get_properties, md_trigger_translation, docs_get_viewables, issues_pin_element, and all three webhooks tools (a hook is addressed by a folder URN or a hook id, neither of which maps to a DM project)

Webhook callback allow-list (FORMA_ALLOWED_CALLBACK_HOSTS) constraining where webhooks_create may direct Autodesk to send event data. A callback URL must be https and publicly routable: loopback, private and link-local addresses are refused unconditionally, both because Autodesk cannot deliver to them and to keep the hook from silently targeting infrastructure adjacent to the server. The dry-run preview for webhooks_create states the egress explicitly — what data class flows, to which host, and that deleting the hook is the only way to stop it — so the approving operator sees the ongoing consequence, not just the API call
Read-only mode toggle to globally disable all mutating tools

Per-tool, per-project hourly rate limits covering every project-scoped mutating tool (defaults built in; overridable via FORMA_RATE_CONFIG_PATH). md_trigger_translation is not rate-limited: it is addressed by URN and has no project id to bucket a counter on. The same applies to webhooks_delete, which is addressed by hook id alone, and to a folder-scoped webhooks_create; a project-scoped (Issues) webhooks_create is rate-limited normally

SHA-256 hash-chained local audit log, independently verifiable via meta_verify_audit_chain. An entry is attempted for every tool invocation that reaches a tool handler, at whatever outcome it reaches: preview, executed, idempotent_replay, failed_api, outcome_unknown, or a specific denial reason (denied_readonly, denied_allowlist, denied_rate_limit, denied_business_rule, denied_auth_mode, denied_missing_approval, denied_approval, denied_idempotency). Three limits are disclosed rather than implied: a request rejected by the MCP SDK's own input-schema validation, or naming an unknown tool, is refused by the protocol layer before any handler runs and so is not audited (no Autodesk data is touched on that path); FORMA_AUDIT_INCLUDE_READS=false excludes read-tool calls (mutation calls are always attempted); and audit writes are fail-open by default — a write failure is logged and the call still proceeds — unless FORMA_AUDIT_FAIL_CLOSED=true is set, which aborts the call instead. The log records only a SHA-256 fingerprint of an approval token, never the live token
